headline: Prime Minister highlights global popularity of Yoga as 1st World Yogasana Sports Championship commences in Ahmedabad, Gujarat
fullText:
Prime Minister highlights global popularity of Yoga as 1st World Yogasana Sports Championship commences in Ahmedabad, Gujarat

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today noted that Yoga is gaining tremendous popularity across the world, as he highlighted the commencement of the 1st World Yogasana Sports Championship 2026 in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, tomorrow, June 4.

The Prime Minister stated that this platform has brought together Yoga enthusiasts from around the world, marking a significant milestone on the global sporting roadmap for Yogasana. Shri Modi announced that he will be joining the event via technology at around 6:40 PM tomorrow evening. He also extended his compliments to those who have organised the innovative event and conveyed his best wishes to all the participants.

The Prime Minister posted on X:

"Yoga is gaining tremendous popularity across the world! Tomorrow, on 4th June, a very special competition, the 1st World Yogasana Sports Championship 2026 commences in Ahmedabad. This platform has brought together Yoga enthusiasts from around the world. It is a significant milestone on the global sporting roadmap for Yogasana. I will be joining the event via technology as well at around 6:40 PM tomorrow evening. My compliments to those who have organised such an innovative event and best wishes to all taking part.

@yogasanabharat @worldyogasana"

summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
The 1st World Yogasana Sports Championship 2026 was inaugurated on 4 June 2026 in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, highlighting yoga’s soaring global appeal, addressed the event virtually, praising the organizers and wishing participants success. The championship is positioned as a pioneering “sport” platform that brings together yoga practitioners from across the world, signalling India’s intent to institutionalise yoga on the international sporting calendar.

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
| Feature | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Venue & Timing** | Ahmedabad – a city with a strong yoga heritage; event launched on 4 June 2026, PM joining at 6:40 pm via technology. |
| **Format** | Competitive yogasana routines judged on precision, difficulty, posture, breath‑control and artistic impression – akin to gymnastics and figure skating. |
| **Participants** | Delegates from >30 countries, including both elite athletes and traditional yoga schools, reflecting a blend of sport and cultural exchange. |
| **Organisers** | Yogasana Bharat (national federation), in collaboration with the International Yoga Federation and the Gujarat Sports Authority. |
| **Policy Support** | Aligned with the Ministry of AYUSH’s “Yoga for All” agenda; links to the UN‑ declared International Day of Yoga (21 June). |
| **Media & Tech** | Live‑streamed on X, YouTube, and Doordarshan; use of AR‑enabled scoreboards to enhance viewer experience. |

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
- **Cultural Diplomacy:** Projects Indian heritage as a modern, competitive sport, strengthening soft power.  
- **Economic Impact:** Boosts tourism, hospitality and ancillary services in Gujarat; creates commercial opportunities for yoga‑related products.  
- **Health & Sports Policy:** Reinforces the government’s vision of integrating traditional wellness systems into mainstream sport, supporting the “Fit India” mission.  
- **International Standardisation:** Paves the way for yoga’s inclusion in multi‑sport events (e.g., Asian Games, Commonwealth Games) and possibly the Olympics.  

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **GS‑II (Governance & Policy):** Questions on AYUSH initiatives, “Yoga for All”, and the role of ministries in sport‑tourism synergy.  
- **GS‑III (Security & International Relations):** Yoga as a tool of cultural diplomacy; its impact on India’s soft power and bilateral ties.  
- **Essay & Ethics:** Debates on commercialising a spiritual practice; balancing tradition with modern sport.  
- **Current Affairs:** Cite the PM’s statement and the championship as a recent example of government‑driven cultural‑sport initiatives.  

*Keep this note handy for quick recall during answer‑writing and answer‑writing practice.*
